headline: EIA Process Approved 94% of Projects in 2024
context: DOE data obtained via parliamentary question revealed that 94% of Environmental Impact Assessments submitted in 2024 were approved, raising questions about the process's independence.

cards.big: The EIA process is supposed to be an independent environmental safeguard.
cards.sub: When 94% of applications are approved, the question is whether the process filters anything at all.

cards.big: EIA consultants are hired and paid by the project proponent.
cards.sub: This structural conflict of interest has been noted by the Auditor General in 2018, 2021, and 2024.

cards.big: Public participation is limited to a 30-day display period.
cards.sub: Comments are advisory only. DOE is not legally required to address or respond to objections.

cards.big: South Korea's approval rate is 71%. Australia's is 68%.
cards.sub: Malaysia's 94% rate is the highest among comparable middle-income countries surveyed by UNEP.

cards.big: An assessment that approves almost everything assesses almost nothing.
EIA reform requires separating the consultant from the proponent, making public objections binding in defined circumstances, and publishing rejection reasons.
